commands.review_summary:
The PR adds persisted skill trust cards, a public trust-card API route, CLI verification output, docs, and regression coverage for skill trust metadata.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. The changed behavior has source-level evidence and PR-body local live-output proof instead of a current-main failure reproduction.
commands.review_followup: This open feature PR needs maintainer review of the new public trust-card contract rather than a narrow automated repair branch.

target.title: Add skill trust cards for ClawHub skills
target.body:
## Summary

Inspired by NVIDIA's Verified Agent Skills write-up:
https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-verified-agent-skills-provide-capability-governance-for-ai-agents/

This adds a ClawHub-native skill trust card so each newly published skill version has a machine-readable release record for installers, automation, and reviewers.

- Stores `clawhub.skill.trust-card.v1` on `skillVersions` with subject, publisher, artifact fingerprint/files/license, capability metadata, static audit summary, and signature status.
- Adds `GET /api/v1/skills/:slug/trust-card` with `?version=` / `?tag=` selection.
- Includes trust cards on skill detail, version detail, and scan responses.
- Adds `clawhub skill verify <slug>` for human or JSON trust-card inspection.
- Refreshes trust-card audit fields when static or dependency-registry scan status changes.
- Keeps provenance fail-closed: source is emitted only from server-resolved GitHub import provenance, not author-controlled frontmatter or generic client publish payloads.

Companion OpenClaw PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85102

## Why

The NVIDIA pattern is useful because agent capability governance needs a stable artifact separate from UI prose. ClawHub already has the raw ingredients: file hashes, publisher identity, declared capabilities, static findings, and later signing state. This PR packages those facts into one API/CLI surface so OpenClaw can ask ClawHub, "what exactly am I about to run?"

## Verification

- `bunx convex codegen`
- `bun run test convex/lib/skillTrustCard.test.ts convex/skills.rateLimit.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts`
- `bun run --cwd packages/clawhub test:src src/cli/commands/inspect.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `bunx tsc -p packages/clawhub/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `bun run format:check`
- `bun run lint:oxlint`
- `git diff --check`
- Autoreview: clean after fixing provenance findings.

## E2E / live notes

Public-live check: `https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/skills/openclaw/trust-card` currently returns 404 because this route is not deployed yet.

Dev deployment check: `https://tame-koala-511.convex.site/api/v1/skills` is reachable, but currently has no seeded skills, so the new trust-card route exists but has no live skill version to verify.

Local contract e2e: ran `bun packages/clawhub/src/cli.ts --registry http://127.0.0.1:<port> skill verify calendar --version 1.2.3` against a live local HTTP server serving the new trust-card response. Observed `Audit: PASS` and request `GET /api/v1/skills/calendar/trust-card?version=1.2.3`.
target.author: steipete
